---
title: "Kworld ATSC-110 - QAM Scan"
date: 2007-05-14
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by Apollo729 on 2007-05-14
I got the Kworld ATSC-110 and am having some issues w/ QAM.

When I do a scan for the QAM Clear channels that I know to be there, I get nothing, well sort of, I get this:

```
jason@ubuntu710x64:~/Desktop$ scan -n limited 
scanning limited
using '/dev/dvb/adapter0/frontend0' and '/dev/dvb/adapter0/demux0'
>>> tune to: 711000000:QAM_256
WARNING: filter timeout pid 0x0000
WARNING: filter timeout pid 0x1ffb
>>> tune to: 717000000:QAM_256
WARNING: filter timeout pid 0x0000
WARNING: filter timeout pid 0x1ffb
dumping lists (0 services)
Done.
jason@ubuntu710x64:~/Desktop$ 

```

There should be 5 channels there, ABC, NBC, FOX, CBS, and a sub of ABC which is just weather.  I know they are there and in the clear, I just can not for the life of me tune to them.

Femon shows a lock on something:

```
jason@ubuntu710x64:~/Desktop$ femon
using '/dev/dvb/adapter0/frontend0'
FE: Nextwave NXT200X VSB/QAM frontend (TERRESTRIAL)
status 1f | signal fe30 | snr e53a | ber 00007ff8 | unc 00000000 | FE_HAS_LOCK
status 1f | signal fe30 | snr e4dc | ber 00000150 | unc 00000000 | FE_HAS_LOCK
status 1f | signal fe00 | snr e53a | ber 00000108 | unc 00000000 | FE_HAS_LOCK
status 1f | signal fe30 | snr e47e | ber 000002a8 | unc 00000000 | FE_HAS_LOCK
status 1f | signal fe30 | snr e4ac | ber 00000150 | unc 00000000 | FE_HAS_LOCK

jason@ubuntu710x64:~/Desktop$ 

```

I am on Comcast, off the Williamsport, PA area, this worked before for me, but I had no sound, I assumed it was a issue on there end, and lately I noticed the PID had changed for the locals so I thought I would try again, however this time I get nothing, and the only thing that has changed is that I am running 7.04.

This is what my scan showed before:

---

### Post by modorf on 2007-05-23
Scan for all QAM channels for digital cable:
scan -U A 2 /usr/share/doc/dvb-utils/examples/scan/atsc/us-Cable-HRC-center-frequencies-QAM256 > channels.conf.org

look through the file to make sure none of the station names have spaces.
awk the file to remove '[' ']':
cat channels.conf.orig | awk '{ sub (/\[/,""); sub (/\]/,""); print $1;}' > channels.conf

---
